data.title: Lyft and Tensor Robocars to make consumer-owned AVs available on Lyft platform
data.description:
To keep up with competitors that are gaining ground in the robotaxi space, Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) is partnering with Tensor Auto to deploy consumer-owned robotaxis in Europe and North America beginning in 2027 on the Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) platform.  

Tensor Robocars will be available for private ownership, enabling owners to generate income through the Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) platform, in addition to providing an additional source of revenue for the ride-share company.

By introducing Tensor autonomous vehicles to its network and reserving hundreds for its own fleet, Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) is working to close the gap with rival Uber (UBER), which has accelerated its autonomous ambitions through partnerships with Waymo (GOOG), Momenta, WeRide (WRD), and Baidu (BIDU).

As the first company to develop a “Level 4 AV” designed for personal ownership, the Tensor Robocar provides an avenue to monetize robotaxis for individuals, representing an “important milestone in AV adoption.”

“Lyft has created opportunities for millions of people to earn on the platform, but right now, one of the last barriers to rideshare is time — you have to physically be in your vehicle to give rides and earn," said Jeremy Bird, Executive Vice President of Driver Experience at Lyft.

"Future owners will be able to start earning on the Lyft network the moment their vehicle rolls off the lot, while riders get access to some of the most advanced and luxurious AVs on the road. This is exactly the kind of innovation that expands what's possible in transportation."

Tensor Robocars feature L4 technology, powered by eight Nvidia (NVDA) DRIVE AGX Thor SoCs, with over 100 sensors within an agentic AI architecture that can process over 53 gigabits of sensor data per second.

Over the next few years, Tensor Robocars will be deployed across the U.S., Europe, and the Middle East to immediately onboard on Lyft’s (LYFT) platform and give owners instant access to the vehicle in markets where “Lyft-ready” L4 technology is available.

Lyft (LYFT) shares were initially nudged higher on the news before surrendering gains and retreating back into negative turf for a fifth straight day. 

Source: Press Release

data.title: LPL Financial raised to Market Perform at Raymond James
data.description:
Raymond James upgraded LPL Financial Holdings (NASDAQ:LPLA) to Market Perform from Underperform as analyst Wilma Burdis expects the fed funds target rate would have to fall "materially below 3% in 2026 to significantly pressure LPLA's intermediate-term earnings."

LPL's (NASDAQ:LPLA) Commonwealth acquisition is seen boosting earnings and diversifying away from client cash income; however, its organic net new asset growth may be more modest as it focuses on the Commonwealth acquisition, the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

Rich Steinmeier, who became CEO in October 2024, has increased focus on controlling general & administrative expenses, compared with his predecessor. "Core G&A is now guided to grow ~25% at the midpoint after the Commonwealth transaction for full-year 2025 year-over-year, while we estimate total advisory and brokerage assets are set to grow ~38%," Burdis wrote.

Raymond James lowered its Q3 2025 EPS estimate for LPLA to $4.40 from its prior estimate of $4.56. Its full-year 2025 EPS estimate dropped to $18.81 from $19.17, and its full-year 2026 EPS estimate was trimmed to $22.57 from $22.72.

LPL Financial (LPLA) stock rose 0.5% in Thursday morning trading.

The Market Perform rating sits between the SA Quant rating of Sell and the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Hershey's new products include a Reese's Oreo Cup concoction
data.description:
Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) issued a sneak peek at a number of products it plans to formally unveil at the NACS Show next week in Chicago.

The new product innovations include Reese's Oreo Cup, Jolly Rancher Ropes Tropical Flavor, Shaq-A-Licious  XL Gummies in Sneaker Shapes, Dot's Homestyle Pretzels Buffalo Flavor, and reformulated SkinnyPop Popcorn White Cheddar and Butter.

The company noted that fast-growing brands like Jolly Rancher, Shaq-A-Licious XL Gummies, and the newly acquired Sour Strips are propelling its non-chocolate confectionery growth, with Sour Strips expanding distribution in 2026. Strategic acquisitions were said to be boosting Hershey's (NYSE:HSY) appeal among Gen Z shoppers and strengthening its position in convenience retail. 

In addition, Hershey (HSAY) said its "Everyday Multiples" strategy bundles low-cost items to encourage shoppers to buy more, offering consistent value and using prominent displays and signage to drive awareness. The approach was noted to be highly effective in convenience stores, where confectionery promotions deliver a 56% average unit lift and standard bars achieve a 92% promotional lift.

Shares of Hershey (HSY) were down 1.6% in morning trading.

data.title: Rail Vision signs term sheet with acquisition of 51% stake in Quantum Transportation
data.description:
• Rail Vision (NASDAQ:RVSN) stated on Thursday the signing of a non-binding term sheet for a strategic transaction to acquire 51% ownership of Quantum Transportation.
  • Both companies are set to combine quantum AI with advanced vision and safety technologies that will grow Rail Vision’s future product lines and deliver long-term value for stakeholders.
  • The company will issue shares approximately 4.99% of its share capital in exchange for their full holdings in Quantum Transportation.
  • Further, the company will extend a convertible loan of up to $0.7M to Quantum Transportation at an 8% annual interest rate to support ongoing operations and development.

data.title: Cineplex reports september box office results
data.description:
• Cineplex (CGX:CA) announced on Thursday box office revenues of $37.7M for September 2025, led by a strong opening for the movie Conjuring: Last Rites.
  • The movie led the month with an $84M debut, marking the third-largest opening for any horror film in history. 
  • While July 2025 box office revenues were at par with July 2024, August 2025 saw a major slump compared to last year, dragging the Q3 revenues to $159.4M compared to $174.8M in the previous year.

data.title: Citizens Financial stock upgraded at BofA Securities on 25% upside potential
data.description:
Citizens Financial (NYSE:CFG) was trading higher after BofA Securities upgraded the banking stock on a 25% upside potential.

Shares were +0.96% to $52.51 before the opening bell on Thursday.

"We upgrade Citizens Financial (NYSE:CFG) to Buy from Neutral, raise PO to $65 from $52, now based on 12.5x our 2026 P/E and 1.6x our 2026 P/TBV (vs 10x/1.3x prior) implying 25% potential upside," said analysts including Ebrahim Poonawala and Brandon Berman.

"While we have missed the stock’s initial outperformance (1700bp YTD vs. super regional bank peers) we think valuation remains compelling given multiple idiosyncratic catalysts to drive EPS growth," said the research note.

The investment bank estimates over 300 basis points of improvement in the return on tangible common equity by 2028, accelerating pre-provision net revenue growth, and M&A optionality boosting stock valuation.

BofA's rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system sees the stock as Strong Buy.

data.title: Figma partners with Google Cloud to expand AI-powered design tools
data.description:
Figma (NYSE:FIG) on Thursday announced a collaboration with Google Cloud (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) to expand the use of artificial intelligence (AI) across its design and product development platform.

Google’s AI models, including Gemini 2.5 Flash, Gemini 2.0, and Imagen 4, will further help Figma's more than 13 million monthly active users' product design needs, the company said. With Gemini's context windows, AI toolset, data platform, and cybertools, Figma expects to meet the increasing demand for user-friendly AI tools.

“With Gemini on Google Cloud, Figma can more easily help its users make high-quality images with just a prompt; experiment with site functions before launch; and accelerate product development by eliminating tedious workflows that slow the design process down,” Google said in its press release.

Early integrations of Gemini 2.5 Flash helped Figma cut latency by 50% for its "Make Image" feature, delivering faster and more responsive image generation.

Earlier this week, ChatGPT CEO Sam Altman  announced the integration of Figma and other apps on its AI platform, sending the cloud-based design platform’s shares soaring by over 10%.

On Monday, during his keynote speech at DevDay 2025, Altman launched Apps SDK, which will provide access to various apps within the ChatGPT dialogue window.

data.title: Talen Energy announces launch of proposed senior notes offerings
data.description:
• Talen Energy (NASDAQ: TLN) announced on Thursday that Talen Energy Supply, a direct wholly owned subsidiary of TEC, proposed to offer and sell senior notes due 2034 and 2036 in a private offering.
  • The company plans to use the current and new net proceeds of the $1.2M senior secured term loan B credit facility to fund the previously announced acquisitions of the Freedom Energy Center and the Guernsey Power Station.
  • The notes and related guarantees will be offered exclusively to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to non-U.S. persons under the Securities Act.

data.title: Sealed Air upgraded by RBC Capital Markets as cost cuts, food growth drive turnaround
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets late Wednesday upgraded Sealed Air Corporation (NYSE:SEE) to Outperform from Sector Perform, citing stabilizing volumes in its packaging businesses, aggressive cost-cutting initiatives and the potential for meaningful upside in the company’s stock price.

Packaging segments show signs of recovery

Analysts led by Arun Viswanathan expect Sealed Air’s (NYSE:SEE) protective packaging division, which has struggled since 2022, to stabilize by late 2025, with modest growth in industrial and electronics demand helping to lift volumes. Meanwhile, its food segment remains resilient, buoyed by steady demand for fresh protein in both retail and foodservice channels. The firm highlighted gains in Europe and Latin America as signs of competitive momentum.

Aggressive cost savings targeted

The report points to more than $100 million in expected annual savings through Sealed Air’s (SEE) “CTO2Grow” program, which includes supply chain streamlining, reduced general and administrative expenses, and offshoring initiatives. These savings could account for as much as 15–17% of projected earnings per share in fiscal 2025 and 2026. Analysts believe additional efficiency gains may follow in the years ahead.

Valuation and upside potential

RBC raised its price target to $48 from $35, implying more than 30% upside from current levels. The analysts value Sealed Air (SEE) at nine times expected 2026 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, with the potential to reroute free cash flow to share buybacks once net leverage falls to 3 times. In a bullish scenario, shares could climb as high as $59, while downside risks could push them to $33.

Headwinds and risks

The company still faces challenges, including weaker beef consumption in the United States -- a key end-market for its food packaging segment -- and macroeconomic pressures in Europe, where it has significant exposure. Longer-term secular shifts away from red meat could also pose risks to demand.

Strategic reset under way

The analysts noted Sealed Air’s (SEE) push into automation and digitalization, with the goal of having half of its sales online by 2025. Combined with sustainability initiatives -- such as compostable trays and reduced-resin packaging -- the company is positioning itself for incremental growth of 6% to 9% over the long term.
